title: Evaluation Context
description: The fully assembled input to the evaluation engine. Constructed by the system (not the advisor) by resolving the applicant profile, catalog data, and rule set into a self-contained context. The engine should be able to evaluate eligibility using only this document — no additional lookups needed. Supports single-program evaluation and multi-program screening.

applicant.description: The full resolved applicant profile. Copied from the applicant_profile at assembly time so the context is self-contained and the evaluation is reproducible.

evaluation_scope.description: Defines what is being evaluated — a single program or a batch screening across multiple programs.

mode.description: 'single_program' = evaluate one specific program. 'multi_program_screening' = evaluate eligibility across multiple programs and return which ones match.

targets.description: One or more target program evaluations. For 'single_program' mode, this array has exactly one entry.

offering.description: Resolved offering data from program_offerings.json. Null if evaluating general eligibility without a specific intake.

rule_set.description: The full resolved rule set to evaluate against. Embedded so the context is self-contained.

properties._comment: Full rule set structure as defined in rule_set.schema.json is embedded here at runtime

evaluation_date.description: The reference date for the evaluation. Used for: language test expiry checks, age calculations, rule set version selection, deadline comparisons. Typically today's date, but can be set to a future date for planning.

advisor_overrides.description: Advisor-specified overrides that modify how the evaluation is performed. These allow advisors to skip checks (e.g. verbal confirmation pending), force-include rules, or note assumed documents.

skip_categories.description: Rule categories to skip entirely. E.g. ['language_requirement'] if verbal confirmation is pending.

override_reasons.description: Justification for each override, keyed by category or rule_id. E.g. { 'language_requirement': 'Verbal confirmation from university pending written proof' }.

assumed_documents.description: Documents the advisor knows are coming. E.g. ['ielts_certificate', 'translated_transcript']. The engine may treat these as 'provided' for document checks.

apply_exceptions.description: Whether to apply known exceptions. Set to false to see raw rule results without exception overrides.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this context was assembled

created_by.description: Advisor identifier who requested the evaluation
